result:
**Goal: Add a Time-Travel & Timeline Branching system.**

Pause any running simulation, scrub backward and forward through its full history, then fork an alternate timeline from any past frame — change a rule, draw new cells, or tweak a parameter — and watch both the original and the branched timeline evolve side-by-side. This turns the platform from a "watch and record" tool into a true exploration instrument for understanding emergent behavior: "what if I had removed that glider at frame 200?" gets a real answer.

Concretely: ring-buffer frame history (configurable depth), keyboard-driven scrub (left/right arrows in time-travel mode), a fork command that snapshots the current historical frame into a parallel simulation, and a split-view that shows original vs. branch evolving in lockstep. Builds naturally on the existing recording infrastructure, comparison mode, and the analytics overlay.
